admin_danger_action_full_reset_descripti: Runs every action above and additionally deletes all non-protected users. Preserves only: you, the bootstrap admin, the sentinel user, and game type seed data. Equivalent to a freshly booted container.
admin_danger_action_wipe_game_history_de: Deletes every room, round, submission, and vote. Preserves packs, users, invites, and object storage.
admin_danger_action_wipe_invites_descrip: Deletes every invite token. Existing users stay logged in and keep their account — this only affects future sign-ups.
admin_danger_action_wipe_packs_descripti: Deletes every pack, every item, every pack media object, AND all game history that depends on them (rooms, rounds, submissions, votes). Empties the entire object storage bucket.
admin_danger_action_wipe_sessions_descri: Deletes every session and every magic link token except your own. All other users are bounced to login on their next request. Your session is preserved.
admin_danger_intro: These actions permanently delete data and cannot be undone. They are disabled in production. Use them in dev and preprod to reset the system to a known state.
admin_game_types_readonly_hint: Read-only — game types are registered in code.
admin_health_runbook_postgres: Likely causes: connection pool exhausted, slow query holding locks, or the DB container restarting. Check `docker compose logs postgres` and `pg_stat_activity`.
admin_health_runbook_rustfs: Likely causes: RustFS container crashed, network between backend ↔ pangolin broken, or credentials rotated. Verify RUSTFS_ENDPOINT reachable and the bucket exists.
admin_health_runbook_smtp: Likely causes: SMTP provider rate-limiting, credentials rotated, or relay DNS/TLS misconfig. Dev stacks should hit Mailpit; check SMTP_HOST and outbox toast state.
admin_invites_toast_created_malformed: Invite created, but response was malformed.
admin_network_degraded: Network degraded — backing off to every {seconds}s.

marketing_adv1_body: One Docker Compose stack. Old laptop, home server, €5 VPS — it runs anywhere you run containers.
marketing_adv2_body: No public signup. Single-use invite tokens, optional email restriction. You decide who sits at your table.
marketing_adv3_body: No passwords to leak or rotate. Tokens are SHA-256 hashed in the DB, single-use, expire in 15 minutes.
marketing_adv4_body: Consent capture, one-click data export, admin-driven erasure with sentinel-UUID anonymisation. Retention windows built in.
marketing_adv5_body: Meme captioning is the first handler; trivia, drawing duels, pairs, quickfire slot in as plugins. No schema churn.
marketing_adv6_body: GPLv3. Go backend, SvelteKit frontend. Fork it, rebrand it, rewire it — just don't lock it back up.
marketing_final_body: Pick your lane. No wrong answer — and yes, the Lab Master always eats last.
marketing_final_footer: Hosting needs an account. Joining with a code does not.
marketing_final_lane2_body_guest: One account, unlimited rooms. Packs, players, rounds — all yours.
marketing_footer_tagline: A private, self-hosted party meme game. Built for circles that prefer to keep things inside.
marketing_hero_card1_caption: "my wifi after three people join the voice call"
marketing_hero_card2_caption: "me pretending I read the group chat before replying"
marketing_hero_card3_caption: "dev-ops trying to debug at 2am with one working brain cell"
marketing_hero_card4_caption: "my ISP when I ask for upload speed"
marketing_hero_mark: A party game you host yourself.
marketing_hero_sub: FabDoYouMeme is a self-hosted, invite-only party meme game. Spin up a room, share a 4-letter code, caption the deck, vote on the funniest. No accounts for players. No data leaves your lab.
marketing_hero_title_line2: Then deal your friends a punchline.
marketing_how_body: Three steps, one code, zero friction. Your friends don't sign up — they just show up.
marketing_meta_description: A party meme game for you and your people. Self-hosted, invite-only, GDPR-first. Host a room, share the code, caption, vote, laugh.
marketing_og_description: A party meme game for you and your people. Self-hosted, invite-only, GDPR-first.
marketing_og_title: FabDoYouMeme: a private, self-hosted party meme game
marketing_pack1_body: The default pack for new labs. Lightly spicy, universally legible, safe for in-laws.
marketing_pack2_body: For the group chat that Slacks in Markdown. Sprint retros welcome. CI failures encouraged.
marketing_pack3_body: Your pack. Upload images, write prompts, version it, ship it. The studio is a full-fledged tool.
marketing_packs_body: Start with a system pack or forge your own in the Lab. Every maker can upload images, write prompts, and publish decks to their instance.
marketing_popover_subtitle: No account needed — just the code.
marketing_round_body: Every session plays like a real card table. A timer. A hand. Four captions. One winner. Then shuffle and deal again.
marketing_step1_body: Pick a game, pick a pack, get a 4-letter room code. Everything runs on your box — no cloud in the loop.
marketing_step2_body: They tap the code on their phone. No download, no account, no tracking. Guests get a seat at the table.
marketing_step3_body: Round by round. The best punchline wins the hand. Scoring, timers, and reconnects are on the house.
marketing_table_card1_caption: "when the docker compose finally comes up"
marketing_table_card4_caption: "the raspberry pi that hosts our whole weekend"
marketing_why_body: Most party platforms turn your laughter into somebody's training data. This one doesn't — it's GPLv3, self-hosted, and boring where it counts.
privacy_alert_not_compliant: This page is not GDPR-compliant until they are configured.
privacy_alert_prefix: The following environment variables are not set:
privacy_intro: Plain language, no padding. Only the data actually collected, only the processors actually used. Operator details are injected from environment variables, so each deployment shows its own.
privacy_meta_description: Privacy policy for this FabDoYouMeme instance. GDPR Art. 13 disclosure.
privacy_s10_body: Database backups are retained for 7 days for disaster recovery. If you request erasure, your data is deleted from the live database immediately, but may persist in backups for up to 7 days. This is permitted under GDPR Art. 17(3)(b) (legitimate interest, incident recovery).
privacy_s1_email_suffix: . Use this address for all privacy requests (access, erasure, rectification, objection, complaints).

privacy_s3_submissions_body: captions or answers you submit; visible to room players.
privacy_s3_username_body: displayed in-game and on leaderboards; visible to all players in a room.

privacy_s9_outro: All other data (username, submissions, votes, scores, session, logs, backups) is stored on the operator's own infrastructure and is not shared with any third party.

privacy_s11_body:
When you join a group — by invite code, or at registration through a platform+group invite — some moderation decisions pass from the platform operator to the admin(s) of that group. The operator still runs the infrastructure, still enforces age and SFW/NSFW rules at the platform level, and still holds your account data. But the group admin — another user, not the operator — decides who stays in the group, which content is evicted, and whether the group is labelled SFW or NSFW.

This means: group admins see your membership in their group (username, role, join and last-login timestamps); they do not see your email or any data outside the group. Moderation inside the group is on group admins, not us — if you are kicked, banned, or have content evicted, that's their action. You can report it to us directly only for classification breaches or when the complaint is about the group admin themselves.

Joining an NSFW group requires a one-time age affirmation at join time. Leaving a group removes you from it; rejoining requires a fresh invite. If the group is deleted, its state enters a 30-day recovery window and is then hard-deleted; historical games continue under the normal 2-year rule with deleted references shown as [deleted]. If your account is deleted or banned, you are removed from every group; if you were the sole admin, another member is auto-promoted.

Group admins are separate data controllers for group-level data (GDPR Art. 4(7)). The platform operator is the controller for your account and for the platform-wide SFW/NSFW taxonomy. Group admins and the operator are not joint controllers — each is accountable for their own decisions. For data access or erasure that spans both layers, send a single request to the operator contact email at the top of this page.
